workstreams.description: Find exports, functions, and variables that are never imported or called
Find duplicated logic that should be extracted into shared utilities
Find overly complex functions that should be simplified or split
Find exports that are never consumed outside their own module
Find architectural violations: wrong-direction imports, tight coupling, missing abstractions
Find security, correctness, and regression risk issues to harden

Verify feature correctness: logic bugs, incorrect calculations, wrong business logic, missing edge case handling, silent failures, incorrect state transitions, off-by-one errors, incorrect conditionals
Find performance issues: N+1 patterns, unnecessary re-renders, inefficient data structures, blocking operations, missing memoization, over-fetching, expensive computations in render paths

Find code quality and refactoring opportunities: SOLID violations, god objects, files that are too large to reason about, SRP violations, missing abstractions that would simplify multiple callsites, DRY/YAGNI/LoD principle breaches

findings.description: `checkApiHealth` is an exported runtime helper with no references outside or inside its own declaration site. The repo-wide `rg` search only finds the export line, so this is dead executable code rather than just an unnecessary export.
findings.recommended_action: Delete `checkApiHealth` from `frontend/src/api.ts`; if a health probe is still intended, add a real caller before keeping the helper.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/App.tsx`, `frontend/src/ui/ConnectionStatusBanner.tsx`, `frontend/src/ui/GitHubRepoPicker.tsx`, and `frontend/src/ui/MainMenu.tsx` all import `* as api`, yet `rg` still finds no `checkApiHealth` member access anywhere.

Health-adjacent helpers in the same module are live, for example `primePublicWriteSession()` is called from `frontend/src/ui/ConnectionStatusBanner.tsx`, so this is an isolated orphan export rather than an unused module.

findings.description: `fetchDistrictRoom` is an exported runtime helper that is never called anywhere in the repo. The only repo-wide hit is its own declaration, which makes it dead executable code.
findings.evidence: {'code_snippet': 'export async function fetchDistrictRoom(districtId: string): Promise<RoomState | null> {\n    return apiFetch<RoomState>(`/api/districts/${encodeURIComponent(districtId)}/room`);\n}', 'rg_command': "rg --glob '!node_modules' -n -w 'fetchDistrictRoom' .", 'rg_output': './frontend/src/api.ts:542:export async function fetchDistrictRoom(districtId: string): Promise<RoomState | null> {'}
findings.recommended_action: Delete `fetchDistrictRoom` from `frontend/src/api.ts`; if an initial room read is still needed, wire it into the presence flow instead of keeping an unused wrapper.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/App.tsx` imports `* as api` and actively calls `api.districtHeartbeat(...)` for room presence updates, but never `api.fetchDistrictRoom(...)`.
The dead symbol sits in the same durable-object section as the live `districtHeartbeat` helper, so the unused part is the initial fetch wrapper rather than the district-room feature itself.

findings.description: `SnapshotFreshnessCopy` is exported but never referenced outside `snapshotFreshness.ts`. It only names the local return shape for `buildSnapshotFreshnessCopy` and `useSnapshotFreshnessCopy`, so the exported type is dead public surface.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `SnapshotFreshnessCopy`, or inline the object return type into the two local helpers if you do not want a named local interface.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/ui/MainMenu.tsx` and `frontend/src/ui/HUD.tsx` import `useSnapshotFreshnessCopy`, but neither imports `SnapshotFreshnessCopy`.
`frontend/src/ui/HUD.tsx` does import `type SnapshotSource` from the same module, which shows that this module's shared type surface is used selectively and this interface is not.

findings.description: `GitHubReadableRepoListResponse` is exported but never crosses a file boundary. It only appears in `api.ts` as the return type of `fetchGitHubReadableRepos`, so it is dead API surface rather than a live shared contract.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `GitHubReadableRepoListResponse` and keep it local to `api.ts`, or inline the response shape into `fetchGitHubReadableRepos`.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/ui/GitHubRepoPicker.tsx` calls `api.fetchGitHubReadableRepos(...)` but imports only `* as api`, so the wrapper type is not shared at the call site.

`GitHubReadableRepo` from the same module is imported in `frontend/src/store/gameStore.ts`, `frontend/src/localSmokeBridge.ts`, and `frontend/src/ui/selectedRepoStatusCopy.ts`, which makes this unused wrapper a specific leftover rather than an unused module.

findings.description: `RepoRefreshStatusCopy` is exported but only used within `repoRefreshCopy.ts` to annotate the return type of `buildRepoRefreshStatusCopy`. No external file imports the interface, so the export is dead surface area.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `RepoRefreshStatusCopy`, or inline the return type of `buildRepoRefreshStatusCopy` if you do not need a named local interface.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/ui/MainMenu.tsx` imports `buildRepoRefreshStatusCopy` and consumes the returned object directly; it never imports `RepoRefreshStatusCopy`.
`frontend/src/ui/HUD.tsx` imports `buildRepoHudRefreshNotice` from the same module, but no file imports `RepoRefreshStatusCopy`, so only the producer module knows the type name.

findings.description: `SelectedRepoStatusCopy` is exported but never referenced outside `selectedRepoStatusCopy.ts`. It only names the local return shape of `buildSelectedRepoStatusCopy`, so the export is dead API surface.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `SelectedRepoStatusCopy`, or inline the return type in `buildSelectedRepoStatusCopy` if you want to keep the shape purely local.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/ui/MainMenu.tsx` and `frontend/src/ui/GitHubRepoPicker.tsx` both import `buildSelectedRepoStatusCopy`, but neither imports `SelectedRepoStatusCopy`.
The same file already keeps `SelectedGitHubRepoIngestStateLike` private, which matches the same 'helper-only shape stays local' pattern.

findings.description: `GitHubTokenExchangeResponse` is exported but only used inside `api.ts` as the return and generic type for `exchangeGitHubOAuthCode`. No external file references the interface, so the exported type name is dead contract surface.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `GitHubTokenExchangeResponse` and keep it local to `api.ts`, or inline the response shape into `exchangeGitHubOAuthCode`.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/App.tsx` calls `api.exchangeGitHubOAuthCode(...)` during OAuth callback handling but never imports this interface.

Other `api.ts` types such as `GitHubReadableRepo` and `ApiRuntimeStatus` are actually imported elsewhere, which makes this unused response wrapper a specific leftover rather than evidence that the whole type surface is dead.

findings.description: `RepoRefreshResponse` is exported but never referenced outside `api.ts`. It only annotates the local `refreshGitHubRepo` implementation, so the exported type name is dead public surface.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `RepoRefreshResponse` and keep it local to `api.ts`, or inline the response shape inside `refreshGitHubRepo`.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/ui/MainMenu.tsx` calls `api.refreshGitHubRepo(...)` without importing `RepoRefreshResponse`, so callers already rely on inference.
The shared refresh contract lives in `shared/repoRefresh.ts` and is imported into `api.ts`; this local wrapper is not the cross-file refresh type the rest of the app uses.

findings.description: `RepoRefreshNotice` is exported but only referenced inside `repoRefreshCopy.ts` as the return type of `buildRepoHudRefreshNotice`. No other file imports it, so the exported interface is dead surface area.
findings.recommended_action: Remove `export` from `RepoRefreshNotice`, or inline the return shape of `buildRepoHudRefreshNotice` if you want to avoid a named local interface.
edge_cases: `frontend/src/ui/HUD.tsx` imports `buildRepoHudRefreshNotice` and consumes the returned object shape directly; it never imports `RepoRefreshNotice`.
`ConnectedRepoRefreshStatus` from `shared/repoRefresh.ts` is imported into this file and across the UI, which makes this extra exported wrapper type the unused part.

findings.description: Heat-status classification is duplicated between the HUD and Repo City surface, and the copies have already diverged: the HUD switches to `watch` at 30 while the surface waits until 35.
findings.recommended_action: Extract one shared heat-status helper that returns the label and reuse it from both HUD and surface rendering.
edge_cases: A district with `heatLevel` 30-34 is shown as `watch` in the HUD (`districtStatusLabel`) but `stable` on the surface (`heatStatusLabel` and `heatStatusClassName`).
When `repoCityTransit` is active, both components are describing the same queued destination district, so the mismatch stays visible before the player even arrives.

findings.description: Transit path-length accumulation is implemented three times across HUD, Repo City surface, and the store, with each copy hand-rolling the same loop over path points.
findings.recommended_action: Move path-distance calculation into one shared utility and adapt callers to a single normalized point shape.
edge_cases: HUD builds `remainingTransitPath` from `pathPoints.slice(pathIndex)` plus `targetPosition`, while RepoCitySurface separately maps points into `{x,y}` objects; if one copy starts including or excluding the terminal point differently, the two overlays will disagree on remaining distance.

The store reuses the same math when evaluating route quality in `buildRoadGuidedTransitPath`, so a future bug fix in one copy can change routing behavior without updating the distance the player sees in HUD or surface UI.

findings.description: Current-waypoint distance calculation and `m` formatting are duplicated across the 3D waypoint label, the mission objective tracker, and the HUD direction arrow.
findings.recommended_action: Centralize waypoint-distance calculation and formatting so every mission UI surface reads from the same helper.
edge_cases: If the rounding threshold changes, the floating waypoint label, the objective tracker, and the HUD arrow can show different distances for the same active waypoint.

All three copies currently ignore the Y axis and handle missing waypoints differently (`return null`, `if (currentWp)`, `if (dist < 5) return null`), so adding vertical movement or alternate waypoint shapes would require synchronized fixes in multiple components.

findings.description: Mission triggers and active waypoints build near-identical repo-city markers: the same `calmColor` tint helper and the same ring/octahedron/beam composition are duplicated with only constant tweaks.
findings.evidence:
frontend/src/game/MissionTrigger.tsx:13-15,69-108
function calmColor(source: string, amount: number) {
    return `#${new THREE.Color(source).lerp(new THREE.Color('#c5d3e2'), amount).getHexString()}`;
}
...
<mesh ref={ringRef} rotation={[-Math.PI / 2, 0, 0]} position={[0, 0.05, 0]}>
    <ringGeometry args={[radius - 0.3, radius, 32]} />
    <meshStandardMaterial color={markerColor} emissive={glowColor} ... />
</mesh>
<mesh position={[0, 0.5, 0]}>
    <octahedronGeometry args={[0.4, 0]} />
    <meshStandardMaterial color={markerColor} emissive={glowColor} ... />
</mesh>
<mesh position={[0, 5, 0]}>
    <cylinderGeometry args={[0.03, 0.03, 10, 8]} />
    <meshStandardMaterial color={glowColor} emissive={glowColor} ... />
</mesh>

frontend/src/game/Waypoint.tsx:15-17,80-137
function calmColor(source: string, amount: number) {
    return `#${new THREE.Color(source).lerp(new THREE.Color('#c5d3e2'), amount).getHexString()}`;
}
...
<mesh ref={ringRef} rotation={[-Math.PI / 2, 0, 0]} position={[0, 0.06, 0]}>
    <ringGeometry args={[waypoint.radius - 0.5, waypoint.radius, 48]} />
    <meshStandardMaterial color={markerColor} emissive={beamColor} ... />
</mesh>
<group ref={markerRef} position={[0, 2.5, 0]}>
    <mesh>
        <octahedronGeometry args={[0.6, 0]} />
        <meshStandardMaterial color={markerColor} emissive={beamColor} ... />
    </mesh>
</group>
<mesh ref={beamRef} position={[0, 8, 0]}>
    <cylinderGeometry args={[0.06, 0.06, 16, 8]} />
    <meshStandardMaterial color={beamColor} emissive={beamColor} ... />
</mesh>
findings.recommended_action: Extract a shared repo-city marker primitive or a shared tint helper so visual tuning lands in one place.
edge_cases: Any repo-city art-direction change to opacity, emissive intensity, or `DoubleSide` handling has to be repeated in both files or district mission triggers and active waypoints will drift visually in the same scene.
Both components derive visibility from radius-based geometry (`radius` vs `waypoint.radius`), so accessibility tuning for small markers will be easy to apply to one copy and forget in the other.

findings.description: The HUD and main menu each wrap `useSnapshotFreshnessCopy` in nearly identical presentational components that differ mostly by CSS class names and test ids.
findings.recommended_action: Create one shared snapshot-freshness component that accepts a small style variant instead of duplicating the wrapper markup.
edge_cases: The HUD version accepts `generatedAt: string | null | undefined` while the menu version requires `generatedAt: string`, so missing-timestamp handling can diverge if one wrapper changes and the other does not.
If `useSnapshotFreshnessCopy` adds another display field or state, both wrappers need parallel markup updates to keep menu and in-game provenance consistent.

findings.description: Selected GitHub repo status is modeled twice: `selectedRepoStatusCopy.ts` has a shared loading/error/eligible/listed-only state machine, but `MainMenu.tsx` rebuilds the same branches inline for CTA and auth-card copy.
findings.recommended_action: Promote selected-repo UI state into one shared derived model and have MainMenu, the picker, and any status cards render from that single source.
edge_cases: The loading and failure branches both vary on whether `connectedRepo` exists; adding a new fallback path in only one copy will desynchronize the main CTA copy from the picker/status-card copy.

Eligibility policy comes from `repoTranslationEligibility.ts`; if `internal` repos or another visibility state becomes eligible later, both the inline menu logic and the shared status builder must change together to avoid contradictory UI.

findings.description: Bootstrap snapshot parsing and smoke-harness snapshot construction both manually clone and normalize repo snapshots from the same sample JSON, but they do it in separate, already-diverging code paths.
findings.recommended_action: Extract one repo-snapshot normalization/cloning utility and have both bootstrap and smoke harness call it, with explicit options for truncation or commit override.
edge_cases: Bootstrap truncates to `MAX_BOOTSTRAP_MODULES` and filters `dependencyEdges` and `signals` to `allowedModuleIds`, while `buildSmokeConnectedRepo` keeps the full module graph; the same sample snapshot can therefore drive different districts, missions, and signals in normal and smoke flows.
If `RepoModel` gains another nested array or metadata field, both manual clone paths need updates or one environment will silently retain shared references or omit fields that the other preserves.

current_slice_recommendation.title: Scan frontend/src and worker/src for overly complex functions

known_risks: Codex may hallucinate evidence — always verify file_path exists before storing finding
Fix mode is skipped in dry_run mode (no commits) but Codex still runs
hardening workstream (q-006) is deferred until higher-priority workstreams complete at least one cycle
decisions.json in docs/review_artifacts/ can be edited manually to mark findings as keep or defer
